Title: AME Reit to acquire RM50.2mil Johor industrial land in maiden third-party deal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: AME Real Estate Investment Trust (AME Reit) is acquiring three parcels of vacant freehold industrial land measuring a combined 3.59 hectares at i-Park @ Senai Airport City, Johor, for RM50.2 million, marking its first third-party acquisition since its listing.
In a statement today, AME Reit said RHB Trustees Bhd, acting on its behalf, had entered into a sale and purchase agreement (SPA) for the proposed acquisition.
AME REIT, however, did not disclose the identity of the vendor from whom it is acquiring the land.
The transaction marks AME Reit's first third-party acquisition since its listing, following RM289.5 million in completed asset acquisitions from its sponsor, AME Elite Consortium Bhd.
"The investment deepens AME Reit's presence in a strategic industrial hub, where i-Park@SACproperties already comprise 48.6 per cent of its portfolio by value.
"AME Reit's expansion within the mature park leverages its established operational scale and deep market knowledge, with the new parcels situated alongside multinational manufacturing and logistics players," it said.
Chan Wai Leo, chief executive officer and executive director of I Reit Managers Sdn Bhd, the management company of AME Reit, said expanding the Reit's footprint in i-Park @ Senai Airport City would enable it to leverage its established expertise and execution capabilities to accelerate its expansion.
"This transaction also broadens our investment channels, strengthens our portfolio resilience, andreinforces our commitment to deliver sustainable earnings and long-term value to our unitholders," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Four Kelantan mosques hit by donation box thefts
Article:
TUMPAT: Police are investigating the theft of donation boxes from four mosques in the district over a two-day period between July 20 and yesterday.
Tumpat police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Khairi Shafie said the cases occurred at Masjid Al Aziz Al Hadi in Mukim Kota Warisan and Masjid Nurul Hidayah in Kampung Gerong, Palekbang.
He added that the other two mosques involved were Masjid Kampung Kok Keli in Palekbang and Masjid Ar-Rahman in Kubang Batang, Chabang Empat.
He said reports were lodged by the respective mosque committees after they discovered that the donation boxes had gone missing.
"The actual amount of losses has yet to be determined.
"Preliminary investigations revealed that the suspects used the same modus operandi in all four cases by removing the donation boxes from their original locations before prying them open," he said.
Mohd Khairi said police were actively tracking down those responsible for the thefts.
He said all four cases were being investigated under Section 380 of the Penal Code for theft in a building, which carries a maximum prison sentence of 10 years and a fine upon conviction.
He urged anyone with information on the incidents to contact the nearest police station or the Tumpat district police operations room at 09-725 7222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang's 1926 Heritage Hotel reopens
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A century-old heritage property in Penang has reopened as a boutique urban resort, underscoring the growing appeal of heritage-led hospitality as Malaysia's domestic tourism continues to expand.
The 1926 Heritage Hotel Penang, originally built in 1926 as residences for British colonial officials, was officially relaunched on Thursday under The Unlimited Collection by The Ascott Limited after an extensive restoration.
The redevelopment transformed the property into a 78-room hotel spanning 24 interconnected heritage link houses.
Located along Jalan Burma in George Town, the hotel preserves its Anglo-Malay architectural character while offering a heritage-inspired accommodation experience.
Its reopening comes amid increasing investment in heritage and lifestyle hotels across Penang, where operators are repurposing historic buildings to meet rising demand for experiential travel.
The launch also coincides with robust growth in Malaysia's domestic tourism sector.
According to the Department of Statistics Malaysia, domestic tourism reached 290.1 million visitors in 2025, an 11.5 per cent increase from 260.1 million in 2024. Growth continued into the first quarter of 2026, with domestic visitor numbers rising 7.2 per cent year-on-year to 74.7 million.
Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said heritage conservation through adaptive reuse plays an important role in preserving the state's architectural identity while supporting sustainable tourism development.
"Penang continues to strengthen its position as one of the region's leading tourism destinations by embracing developments that highlight our rich identity while meeting the expectations of today's travellers," Chow said in a statement.
Chow said the restoration of the hotel illustrated how adaptive reuse could preserve historic buildings while enhancing the long-term value of the tourism economy.
"Projects like these add depth and distinction to the island while creating new advantages for businesses and communities," he said.
The launch ceremony was attended by Penang Mayor Datuk Rajendran P. Anthony and the hotel's owner, Datuk Frank Koay.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Resilient economy, policy support drive outlook, stronger Q2 growth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's resilient economic outlook, underpinned by targeted government measures, has helped lift second-quarter (Q2) growth above expectations despite heightened global uncertainties, Treasury secretary-general Tan Sri Johan Mahmood Merican said.
The economy expanded 5.8 per cent in the second quarter, according to advance estimates, accelerating from 5.4 per cent in the first quarter as policy measures continued to support domestic activity and business confidence.
Johan said the stronger performance reflected the effectiveness of the government's economic management and targeted initiatives in cushioning the economy against external headwinds.
"This shows that the government's economic management and the measures implemented, such as the targeted diesel subsidy and initiatives to increase financing for SMEs, clearly demonstrate that these measures have helped support the momentum of economic growth and the country's economic stability.
"With that, the government is confident that such measures will continue to ensure that the economy remains resilient despite uncertainties stemming from the global situation."
Johan told reporters after the memorandum of understanding (MoU) signing between the Securities Commission (SC) and Hong Kong's Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) here today.
On preparations for 2027 Budget, Johan said it was still too early to determine the crude oil price assumption that would underpin next year's fiscal projections.
He noted, however, that oil prices are likely to remain elevated while geopolitical tensions in the Middle East persist.
"What is clear is that with the Middle East crisis yet to be resolved, oil prices do not appear likely to ease and are expected to remain higher than pre-crisis levels.
"That is why it is important for the government to continue implementing policies that ensure supply security so that economic activities can continue smoothly," he said.
Johan added that the government would continue monitoring global developments closely and introduce additional measures where necessary to safeguard business continuity and the wellbeing of Malaysians.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Desa Lestari revamp aims for RM2,300 household income
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The government will revamp the Desa Lestari programme, targeting a monthly household income of RM2,300 for participants through a strengthened implementation framework.
Deputy Prime Minister and Rural and Regional Development Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the decade-old programme would undergo more than a rebranding exercise, with reforms aimed at improving governance, expanding market access and delivering sustainable economic gains for rural communities.
The Rural Development Ministers' Council (MEXCLUB) has agreed to strengthen Desa Lestari as a key catalyst for rural economic growth and household income, with a special task force to undertake structured interventions involving participating cooperatives.
The task force will focus on enhancing governance, rebranding efforts, market access and strategic partnerships, while state governments have been urged to expedite land approvals, infrastructure development and inter-agency coordination to unlock the economic potential of rural assets.
"We are not merely rebranding a programme that has been running for the past 10 years. We want to transform its content and implementation because the existing system can be improved.
"It is managed by cooperatives, and their chairmen and board members change quite frequently. When these changes occur, they create constraints in implementation," he told reporters after chairing the 61st MEXCLUB Coordination Meeting today.
To ensure continuity, Zahid said professionally qualified individuals from within villages would be appointed to manage the programme on behalf of cooperatives and communities.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aminuddin: 'I will work '200pct to ensure PH's victory'
Article:
PORT DICKSON: Negri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said he will work "200 per cent" to ensure the coalition's victory.
This, after PH chairman and Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim confirmed him as the menteri besar candidate if the coalition successfully retains control of the state government.
Aminuddin, who is PH's Linggi candidate, said the announcement was not new, as it had already been made several times since the launch of the election machinery.
The prime minister has announced several times that I am the menteri besar candidate.
"So I need to work not just 100 per cent, but 200 per cent together with the entire team to ensure PH's victory.
"I will ensure that we continue moving forward because we see that PH's candidates this time are capable of providing strong competition.
"With their experience and capabilities, they are able to carry out this responsibility well, God willing," he told a press conference here today.
Meanwhile, Aminuddin said PH would not entertain political attacks or allegations it considers defamatory during the remainder of the Negri Sembilan election campaign, choosing instead to focus on explaining its manifesto and policy offerings to the people.
He said the various attacks directed at him, including claims that he is a "DAP puppet," were merely assumptions without any supporting evidence.
"Those attacks do not affect us. What we are offering the people has already been outlined in our manifesto.
"That is far more important than responding to the assumptions and slander being spread by the opposition," he said.
He said PH's slate of candidates this time possesses the experience and capability to continue the state's development agenda if given another mandate.
He also expressed confidence that voters would be able to assess the state government's track record based on its performance and the proposals presented.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hospital sexual harassment claims will not be ignored, says Sabah minister
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah Women, Health and People's Wellbeing Ministry is looking into allegations of  sexual harassment at a hospital here and is coordinating action with the federal authorities.
Minister Datuk Julita Majungki said there is zero tolerance for sexual harassment and  vows the allegations will not be ignored.
""This matter is now under the purview of the Health Ministry and the Sabah Health Department. However, I cannot reveal anything at this stage... but suffice to say that we will not simply ignore it," she told reporters at the Sabah assembly media centre here.
Yesterday, Kepayan assemblyman Chin Teck Ming, from Parti Warisan, and Warisan health bureau chief Dr Istefan Koh  alleged in a social media video that a staff member  at a hospital here had been sexually harassed by a superior.
Sabah Police Commissioner Datuk Jauteh Dikun said  police were investigating an
alleged sexual harassment
incident involving a hospital director   that occurred in October last year.
The incident was only reported in January.
Meanwhile, when debating the District Chief, Native Chief and Headman Bill 2026, Nurulalsah Hassan Alban (Warisan-Sungai Sibuga) said the alleged sexual harassment at the hospital had become a workplace psychological threat for victims.
"A hospital leader with such conduct cannot be relied upon, and there is a systemic failure when the Sabah  health director's office handles transfers or administrative action involving senior officers.
"I truly hope this is not an integrity issue or an abuse of power to protect (others of) their own rank.
"This is related to my oral question about the measures taken by the government to increase the number of doctors.
"If cases like this are not handled properly, we may lose another doctor."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah dismemberment murder accused fit to stand trial
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: A septuagenarian charged with murdering a woman whose dismembered body parts were found in Indah Permai, Sepanggar, has been found fit to stand trial, the magistrate's court heard today.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Akaash Singh informed magistrate Dzul Elmy Yunus of the findings when the case came up for mention to update the court on the status of the psychiatric evaluation report on the accused, Piluta Samad, 72.
Akaash said the report had been completed and concluded that Piluta did not suffer from any mental illness and was fit to enter a plea and stand trial.
He, however, applied for another mention date to obtain further instructions from the prosecution.
The magistrate then fixed Aug 27 at 9am for further mention.
On April 15, the court ordered Piluta to undergo a 30-day psychiatric evaluation at Hospital Mesra Bukit Padang following an application by the defence for a mental assessment under Section 342 of the Criminal Procedure Code.
On May 15, the court granted a one-month extension for the psychiatric evaluation.
Piluta was first brought before the court on Feb 27, when he was charged with murdering his former fiancée, Hamidah Husin @ Lisa, 44.
The offence was allegedly committed between 6pm and 7pm on Feb 11, at a house without a lot number in Kampung Sri Kenangan, Jalan Sepanggar.
He was charged under Section 302 of the Penal Code, which carries the death penalty or jail of not less than 30 years and not more than 40 years upon conviction.
If the death penalty is not imposed, a male offender below the age of 50 is also liable to not fewer than 12 strokes of the cane.
No plea was recorded as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court. Piluta's plea will only be taken after the case is transferred there.
Lawyers Datuk Ram Singh, Joan Goh, Ian Vun and Prem Elmer Ganasan represented the accused.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH, BN in neck-and-neck race for Negri Sembilan, says Vodus Research
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) are locked in a tight race in the Negri Sembilan election, with BN leading in more clear-cut seats while PH holds an edge in closely contested constituencies.
According to Vodus Research's latest forecast, PH leads the statewide popular vote with 42 per cent, ahead of BN at 35 per cent and Perikatan Nasional (PN) at seven per cent.
Independent candidates account for two per cent of the popular vote, while 14 per cent of respondents remain undecided.
Vodus Research founder Dr Kelvin Goh said the current model projects PH winning 17 seats, BN 15 and PN two, with two additional BN-PH contests remaining unresolved.
"While BN holds 15 clear leads compared with PH's nine, PH is currently ahead in eight of the 10 closely contested seats, raising its projected total to 17.
"This remains a very close race. BN has more clear leads, but PH holds a slight advantage once closely contested seats are included.
"This means the final result could still go either way, and a hung assembly cannot be ruled out. Ultimately, campaign performance, turnout and how parties manage the close-seat battleground will determine who reaches 19 seats," he said in a statement.
A simple majority requires 19 of the 36 seats, meaning neither PH nor BN has secured enough to form the state government.
Goh said the final outcome would likely depend on three key factors: BN retaining Malay support amid competition from Bersatu and other alternatives; PH maintaining its advantage among Chinese and other voters while improving turnout; and both parties managing closely contested seats where small shifts in participation or late decisions could alter the result.
He said BN's strongest advantage over PH's popular vote lead lies in its net trust score, with BN recording 45 per cent, more than double PH's 19 per cent, while PN trailed at five per cent.
"The findings suggest BN has a clearer advantage when voters weigh confidence against distrust in a party's ability to manage Negri Sembilan," he said.
Leadership ratings add another dimension, with Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun recording a favourability rating of 32 per cent, significantly lower than the 58 per cent recorded by Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi in Vodus Research's earlier Johor election survey.
Ethnic voting patterns showed varying levels of support.
Among Bumiputera voters, BN leads with 41 per cent, followed by PH at 27 per cent and PN at 25 per cent.
Among Chinese voters, PH leads with 59 per cent, compared with BN at 20 per cent and PN at nine per cent.
Among other voters, PH leads with 42 per cent, followed by BN at 32 per cent and PN at 21 per cent.
Cost of living emerged as the top concern, with 55 per cent selecting it as one of their top three issues.
This was followed by jobs and wages (34 per cent), housing affordability (26 per cent), and corruption and governance (24 per cent).
Other concerns include state economic development, safety and crime, religious and racial harmony, education, local council services and candidate quality.
The forecast also identified a sizeable pool of movable voters, with about 70 per cent saying their choice was either completely or fairly certain, while 30 per cent remained unsure or open to change.
Turnout intention was lower than past elections, with 71 per cent saying they were likely or very likely to vote, but only 51 per cent placing themselves in the strongest "very likely" category.
"Among those at risk of not voting, travel or being outside Negri Sembilan is the most common barrier, followed by inconvenience, work commitments and living outside the voting area," he said.
The survey was conducted among 437 registered Negri Sembilan voters between July 9 and 21.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Betamek's Q1 earnings jump 53pct on stronger sales
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Betamek Bhd's net profit rose 53.2 per cent to RM8.25 million in the first quarter ended June 30, 2026 from RM5.39 million a year ago.
The performance was lifted by higher production volumes across Betamek's automotive product segments, expanding export sales and continued progress in customer diversification.
Revenue for the quarter under review climbed 40.33 per cent to RM79.82 million from RM56.88 million in the same period last year.
The company said its audio and video products remained the largest contributor, generating revenue of RM45.34 million during the quarter, while vehicle accessories contributed RM24.35 million.
Connectivity modules continued to gain traction and contributed RM6.77 million, reflecting Betamek's growing participation in higher electronics-content vehicle platforms.
The compant announced a first interim single-tier dividend of 1.25 sen per ordinary share in respect of financial year 2027 (FY27), payable on Aug 17.
It also recommended a final single-tier dividend of 1.25 sen per share in respect of FY26, subject to shareholders' approval at the forthcoming annual general meeting.
This brings the total dividend for the financial year to six sen per ordinary share, representing a dividend yield of 9.2 per cent.
"We are pleased to begin FY27 on a strong footing, with revenue, gross profit and profit before tax recording healthy growth both year-on-year and quarter-on-quarter.
"The performance was supported by higher production volumes across our automotive product segments and continued progress in our diversification efforts.
"These encouraging developments reflect the strength of our business fundamentals and provide a solid foundation for sustainable long-term growth," said executive director Muhammad Fauzi Abd Ghani in a statement.
Beyond its financial performance, Betamek continued to strengthen its operational and technological capabilities during the quarter.
Its unit Betamek Electronics (M) Sdn Bhd achieved Level 4 recognition under the Industry4WRD Smart Manufacturing Assessment programme by Malaysian Automotive Robotics and IoT Institute.
Meanwhile, the company continues to advance the commercialisation of its SafeSync360 infotainment project while progressing strategic collaborations in electric vehicle communication technologies and next-generation transportation infotainment systems.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Marriott to open Courtyard Penang Icon City in 2026 after the Iconic Hotel revamp
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Marriott International is expanding in Penang with the opening of Courtyard by Marriott Penang Icon City in December 2026, following the transformation of the existing Iconic Hotel Penang.
The global hotelier and Iconic Group have signed an agreement to introduce the Courtyard by Marriott brand through the rebranding of the property, which will undergo a phased upgrade.
The hotel will feature 196 guestrooms and cater to both business and leisure travellers visiting Penang.
Marriott International vice president of hotel development for Asia Pacific excluding China, Andree Susilo, said Malaysia remains a key growth market for the group, driven by strong economic fundamentals and evolving travel demand.
He said this signing reflects the group's commitment to expanding in destinations where strong economic fundamentals and evolving travel demand create long-term opportunities for its owners and brands.
"Our continued collaboration with Iconic Group demonstrates the strength of the relationships we seek to build with owners who share our long-term vision for growth," he said in a statement.
Located within the four-hectare Icon City integrated commercial development in Bukit Mertajam, the hotel is expected to strengthen the area's position as a destination for business and leisure travellers.
Iconic Group chairman Datuk Seri Tan Kean Tet said the signing marked another milestone in the company's partnership with Marriott International.
"Building on the success of our previous collaboration, we look forward to introducing the Courtyard by Marriott brand to Icon City and delivering a hospitality experience backed by Marriott's globally recognized standards," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: Formalise PN-BN proposal if serious
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today said any proposal for cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) should be formally discussed.
The BN chairman said the proposal had only been raised in a campaign speech and had not been discussed through official channels.
"The matter was only mentioned in a speech during the campaign. There has been no discussion.
"It would be better to have a discussion and formalise it if the proposal is serious. But if it is merely campaign rhetoric, then I think the proposal should be properly detailed," he told reporters here today.
Zahid was responding to a question on a reported proposal involving cooperation between PN and BN.
PN has coined the acronym "PBN" to describe its electoral understanding with BN in the Negri Sembilan state election.
Parti Wawasan Negara president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin said the label was intended to avoid confusion among voters.
On a separate issue, Zahid expressed confidence that dissatisfaction among Umno grassroots in Negri Sembilan over the candidacy for the Klawang state seat would be resolved before polling day.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Businesses brace for tougher second half
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian businesses are turning more cautious as rising costs, global uncertainties and supply chain disruptions continue to weigh on operations, according to the Associated Chinese Chambers of Commerce and Industry of Malaysia (ACCCIM).
Its Malaysia Business and Economic Conditions Survey (M-BECS) found that business sentiment weakened in the first half of 2026 and is expected to remain subdued in the second half of the year.
ACCCIM think tank Socio-Economic Research Centre (SERC) executive director Lee Heng Guie said the survey captured the impact of the Middle East conflict on business sentiment, particularly concerns surrounding energy prices and supply chain disruptions.
"The overall picture is that business sentiment has weakened amid cautious expectations. Both the Business Condition Index (BCI) and Business Sentiment Index (BSI) continue to trend lower," he said at the survey findings briefing today.
The survey received 791 responses from companies across a broad range of industries.
Manufacturing, wholesale and retail trade, professional and business services, construction, and hotels and recreation accounted for 69 per cent of respondents.
Lee said SMEs represented 91.4 per cent of surveyed firms, comprising 23.5 per cent micro enterprises and 50.4 per cent small businesses, underscoring the important role of MSMEs in Malaysia's economy.
The BCI, which measures current business conditions, fell 10.5 points to 78.5 in the first half of 2026.
About 34.4 per cent of respondents said economic conditions had worsened, while 34.6 per cent reported a deterioration in the business environment.
Lee said businesses generally maintained a neutral view of the economy, but growing uncertainty surrounding global developments had led to a more cautious outlook.
The BSI, which tracks expectations for the next three to six months, is projected to decline by 12.4 points to 94.9 in the second half of 2026.
Although geopolitical tensions have eased following a 60-day peace arrangement between the US and Iran, businesses remain concerned about disruptions to global energy supplies and key production inputs.
Rising costs remained the biggest challenge, with 51.2 per cent of respondents citing higher raw material prices and 48.2 per cent pointing to increased operating expenses.
"Businesses have been facing persistent cost pressures over the past two years due to various policy and regulatory changes, coupled with rising input costs and ongoing external uncertainties.
"Consumers are comparing prices more carefully, focusing on essential purchases and seeking promotional deals," he said.
The survey found that 50.9 per cent of respondents reported weaker domestic sales in the first half of the year, while 40.6 per cent expect business conditions to remain challenging in the second half.
Production activity also softened, with 42.5 per cent reporting lower output during the first six months of 2026.
Most businesses expect both local and imported input costs to continue rising in the second half of the year.
Access to financing also remains a key concern, particularly for micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs).
"Access to affordable and business-friendly financing remains a pertinent hurdle for MSMEs.
"Many businesses are operating in a higher-cost environment and require funding primarily for working capital and cash-flow management, yet financing costs and approval requirements remain key challenges," Lee said.
Conventional banks remained the primary source of financing, used by 52.9 per cent of respondents, although 24 per cent reported having no financing facilities.
Meanwhile, more than half of respondents said access to financing had become tighter compared with two years ago.
Businesses mainly sought financing for working capital and cash-flow management, reflecting mounting cost pressures and narrowing profit margins.
High financing costs, lengthy approval processes and extensive documentation requirements were identified as the main obstacles to securing bank financing.
Alternative financing channels, including peer-to-peer financing, equity crowdfunding, digital bank financing and venture capital, remained underutilised due to low awareness and limited familiarity among businesses.
Lee said a similar issue existed with development financial institutions, despite generally positive perceptions of their financing rates and terms.
He said greater outreach, simpler application procedures and targeted advisory support were needed to improve awareness and accessibility.
"We saw very low awareness and utilisation of the DFI. The barrier is still related to lack of awareness. Malaysia has a lot of DFIs. The question is why our MSMEs are not fully aware of the facilities offered.
"The issue is not whether financing schemes exist, but whether businesses know about them, understand how to apply, and find the process manageable," said Lee.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid dismisses talk of bid to topple Anwar
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today dismissed speculation of any attempt to unseat Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, reaffirming Barisan Nasional's commitment to the unity government until the end of the 15th Parliament.
Responding to questions over claims of a plot to bring down the government, the BN chairman said there had been various forms of speculation but stressed that he had no intention of supporting any such move.
"I have absolutely no intention. There is no 'Langkah Bengkok', no 'Dubai Move' — nothing of that sort.
"When we committed ourselves to the unity government, following the Yang di-Pertuan Agong's decree, we committed to remain until the end of the 15th Parliament. Full stop," he told reporters here.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police in Wisconsin shoot man dead, online video of killing sparks protests
Article:
MADISON: Police officers in Madison, Wisconsin, fatally shot a man they were trying to arrest on Wednesday during a street-corner scuffle, which was recorded by onlookers and went viral on the internet, sparking protests in the city.
Police said the officer who fired his weapon was injured by a knife that the man produced during the struggle.
Bystander video of the incident circulating online and on news media outlets suggested the man who was shot was a person of colour, and all four officers involved in the incident appeared to be white.
As in many other deadly police shootings in the US in recent years, race added to tensions over whether lethal force was justified, with an African-American community advocacy group saying the man shot on Wisconsin's capital city was Black.
"Madison Police shot another black man. We need all eyes on Madison police. We keep us safe," the group Urban Triage said in a message posted on social media.
Speaking to reporters at a news conference, Madison Police Chief John Patterson described the man who was killed only as a male in his 30s, and declined to specify his race, or the race of any of the four officers - three men and one woman.
Patterson said the incident unfolded when police responded to a call about a person who was suspected of "checking parked vehicles" in the Marquette neighbourhood of Madison, the capital city of Wisconsin.
When police located the man and tried to make contact with him, he fled on a bicycle.
Officers caught up with him about 30 minutes later, and the scuffle ensued after the man "either fell off or was taken off the bicycle by officers," the police chief said.
Patterson said an "independent" investigation would be conducted by the Wisconsin Department of Justice's criminal division, and that all four officers would be placed on administrative leave.
The police chief said he had reviewed two bystander-filmed video clips circulating online, but said there was additional footage that had not been widely seen, and he asked the public not to jump to conclusions.
Asked whether he would classify the shooting as an apparent case of excessive use of force, Patterson answered, "I can't determine that."
The video going viral presents "one perspective" of the incident, he said, adding, "it is not a full overview of everything that was visible from different angles."
The video online shows the four uniformed officers grappling with the man amid shouts of "Let go" and "he's got a knife."
In one clip, an officer is heard yelling "Taser" as police clutch at the man. Seconds later, the man is forced to the ground, and one officer stomps on his legs as another pulls his gun and fires three shots.
That officer then appears to re-holster the weapon and kneel down to the man, who has stopped moving.
Patterson said it appeared to him, also, that there were three shots, and all were fired by a single officer, who he described as a "veteran" of the department.
He said the officer who fired his weapon did so after he had suffered injuries from a "large, fixed-blade knife" that the man had "pulled out or produced" moments before while police were trying to subdue him as he resisted arrest.
A second officer suffered an unspecified injury during the confrontation, according to the police chief.
Between the emergence of the knife and the gunfire, another officer attempted to immobilise the man with a Taser stun gun, but the device, for unknown reasons, proved ineffective, Patterson said.
How much time elapsed between the knife emerging and the shots fired was not clear, he said. But an object that may have been the knife is seen on the ground from the start of the video clip, which did not capture the beginning of the struggle.
News media images showed crowds of demonstrators gathering and marching down the state Capitol in protest after the shooting.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Meta employees' lawsuit shows that if AI fires you, proving it is the hard part
Article:
A novel lawsuit claiming that Meta Platforms relied on discriminatory AI tools to select employees for layoffs highlights the problems workers face in suing employers over the new technology, including proving how it was actually used.
The case helps illustrate why a widely predicted wave of employment lawsuits over AI use has yet to arrive.
Legal experts say workers often have little understanding of how AI systems are used in the workplace and many have also signed away their right to sue in court, agreeing instead to resolve workplace disputes through a private process called arbitration that can keep such claims from ever being tested publicly.
In a ruling last week declining to block Meta from finalising the terminations of 26 people who sued, US District Judge William Orrick identified a fundamental obstacle for plaintiffs who allege that AI discriminated against them: "They were not in the rooms where it happened."
That means workers like the Meta employees, who claim they were targeted for layoffs because they have disabilities or took medical or family leave, often cannot muster the evidence of wrongdoing necessary to quickly secure a win in court.
And they face another obstacle: Like a majority of US workers, the plaintiffs are bound by an arbitration agreement, meaning they cannot band together in a class action, put their case before a jury, or push for a multimillion-dollar settlement in open court.
Companies generally prefer arbitration, which they say is a faster, cheaper alternative to court, while worker advocates say it often favours employers and discourages workers from bringing claims.
The arbitration process is also confidential, so it can shield unfavourable evidence unearthed in an individual case from wider disclosure.
"Even if you establish that a particular system would produce discriminatory outcomes left and right, you have no way of sharing that information with other employees," said Christine Webber, co-chair of the civil rights and employment practice at plaintiffs' firm Cohen Milstein Sellers & Toll.
Webber's firm is not involved in the Meta case. Webber and other plaintiffs' lawyers said those hurdles explain the lack of high-profile court cases involving employers' use of AI even as it becomes routine, and why even the lawsuit against Meta seeking only temporary relief is unusual.
[[nid:1495454]]
One of the few cases to emerge over companies' workplace use of AI tools involves Workday, which is facing claims that its popular HR management software unlawfully filtered out applicants for jobs at other companies based on race, age and disability.
Arbitration is not an issue in that case because Workday does not have agreements with its customers' job applicants. Workday denies the allegations.
The agreements signed by the Meta workers contain a common, narrow exception for seeking a court order that temporarily blocks one side from taking some irreversible action.
But that exception is typically invoked in cases involving the alleged theft of trade secrets or the solicitation of clients or employees, ​and not layoffs of at-will workers.
Orrick denied the plaintiffs a temporary restraining order that would have stopped Meta from completing the layoffs.
He must still decide whether to issue a preliminary injunction, a temporary but longer-lasting order that would put the workers back in their jobs until their individual arbitration cases are resolved.
He said he could change his mind and grant the injunction if the plaintiffs come up with evidence "regarding whether and how AI was used in an improper manner."
A hearing is scheduled for August 24, and the losing side can appeal Orrick's decision.
The plaintiffs claim that in selecting jobs to cut, Meta consulted AI tools that tracked productivity and AI token usage (a measure of how much workers use AI tools), disadvantaging people who missed work because of medical conditions or to care for family members.
They allege that Meta used a number of internal AI-assisted systems including a large language model assistant known as "Metamate," an employee-trained "second brain" that tracked workers' communications and documents, ​and a productivity score drawn from scanning keystrokes, screen content, emails and browser history, according to the lawsuit.
Meta said in court filings and statements last week in response to the lawsuit that humans made all of the decisions concerning nearly 8,000 layoffs announced earlier this year and has denied treating AI usage as a basis for identifying workers to terminate or to conduct performance reviews.
Orrick said in his decision that he was bound to take Meta at its word since the plaintiffs could not present any evidence to rebut those claims.
The plaintiffs' lawyers in a joint statement last week acknowledged the hurdles they face in gathering evidence, even calling on current and former Meta employees to contact them with knowledge of how AI was used in the selection process.
"Meta holds virtually all the relevant information," they said.
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid replies to PM with pantun on 'roof and storm'
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today responded to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's unity-themed pantun with a four-line Malay verse of his own.
He told reporters it was composed shortly after Anwar's post.
Reading the pantun during a press conference here, Zahid said he wrote it at 9.04pm after seeing news reports of the prime minister's posting.
"Mendaki ke Gunung Tahan,
Jatuh ke gaung, temui maut.
Bumbung tercabut, mampu ditahan,
Jika ribut diterbang ke laut."
Yesterday, Anwar shared a four-line pantun on social media carrying a message of unity and consensus.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 3 women charged over viral 'Superman' stunt
Article:
KUANTAN: Three women, including two underage girls, were charged in the magistrate's court today with dangerous riding after performing 'Superman' stunts on their motorcycles along Jalan Kuantan Bypass on July 16.
Nur Liana Atirah Roslan, 21, claimed trial after the charge was read before magistrate Wahidah Zainal Abidin. Her two friends, aged 18 and 17, pleaded guilty.
The trio were separately charged with riding their motorcycles in a manner dangerous to other road users while performing the stunts.
They allegedly committed the offences on their respective Yamaha LC135 motorcycles along Jalan Kuantan Bypass, between Terminal Sentral Kuantan and Bukit Rangin, at about 12.30am on July 16.
They were charged under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, which carries a maximum penalty of five years' jail and a fine of up to RM15,000 upon conviction.
Prosecuting officer Inspector Noraini Mohd Nor handled the prosecution, while all three accused were unrepresented.
Noraini proposed bail of RM5,000 for Nur Liana and requested a new mention date to allow her to appoint a lawyer.
In mitigation, Nur Liana appealed for lower bail, saying she works at an ice cream shop and earns RM2,000 a month.
Wahidah set bail at RM3,000 with one surety and fixed Aug 26 for mention.
Following the guilty pleas by the 18-year-old hotel receptionist and the 17-year-old shop helper, the court set bail at RM3,000 and RM2,000 respectively, and also fixed Aug 26 for mention pending reports from the Welfare Department.
The three women made headlines last week after videos showing them performing
'Superman' stunts
on their motorcycles went viral on social media, prompting a police investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pan Am plane lost for 74 years found on Atlantic seabed
Article:
Some 74 years after a Pan American Airways flight crashed into the water off the coast of Puerto Rico, an aviation search team said it found the wreckage sitting on the ocean floor.
The Air/Sea Heritage Foundation and others, including Discovery Channel's series "Expedition Unknown," found the wreckage of the Pan Am "Clipper Endeavor" at the bottom of the Atlantic Ocean off Puerto Rico's northern coast last month, Discovery said on Tuesday.
The Douglas DC-4 aircraft broke into two sections and came to rest after crashing into the ocean shortly after taking off from Puerto Rico on April 11, 1952, with 64 passengers and five crew members on board.
While everyone on board survived the initial impact, only 12 passengers and five crew members were rescued, with the rest being lost with the plane.
The disaster led to sweeping aviation reforms, with the introduction of pre-flight safety briefings and emergency procedure demonstrations.
"We are all stunned and elated by this discovery yet also humbled to remember what happened in that place so long ago,"  said Russ Matthews, president of the Air/Sea Heritage Foundation.
The search, which involved the Deep Sea Vision company, used high-resolution sonar, with the wreckage being found almost 2,000 feet under the Atlantic Ocean.
The search also involved the use of autonomous underwater drones and ended the mystery of where the aircraft came to rest.--REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Westports Q2 profit jumps 56pct, declares 15 sen dividend
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Westports Holdings Bhd's net profit jumped 56 per cent to RM360.9 million in the second quarter of financial year 2026 (Q2 FY26) from RM231.6 million a year earlier.
Revenue also grew 25 per cent to RM866.9 million from RM691 million, driven by higher tariffs, according to a bourse filing.
The port operator also declared a single-tier first interim dividend of 14.98 sen per share worth RM514.8 million.
Westports' net profit for the six-month period rose 51 per cent to RM687.4 million from RM454 million previously, while revenue surged 34 per cent to RM1.76 billion from RM1.3 billion.
Westports expects its overall container throughput this year to remain broadly in line with last year's level, as easing tensions in the Middle East are expected to support a gradual recovery in global trade conditions despite lingering economic headwinds.
The company said the easing intensity of the Middle East crisis is expected to allow energy supplies to recover quickly, although a return to pre-crisis levels is likely to be more gradual.
It said inflationary pressures that had built up over recent months are also expected to ease, supporting a balance between economic recovery and the lingering effects of higher prices on consumer spending and government fiscal positions.
"The regional transhipment volume could be influenced more by how shipping lines navigate this evolving landscape, as well as by regional supply chain and economic requirements.
"The company currently expects overall container throughput to be roughly the same as the previous year and will provide updated guidance should these fast-evolving conditions materially affect volume outlook," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Six candidates in race to lead UN set for televised debate
Article:
NEW YORK: The six candidates for United Nations secretary-general will debate Thursday in the symbolic General Assembly Hall, one week before the formal start of a selection process crucial to an organisation in deep crisis.
With the campaign lacking a clear favourite and struggling to generate real enthusiasm, the hopefuls will face 90 minutes of questions from diplomats, UN employees and civil society representatives at an event starting at 5 pm (2100 GMT) and broadcast by Bloomberg.
Chile's Michelle Bachelet, Ecuador's Maria Fernanda Espinosa, Rafael Grossi of Argentina, Costa Rican Rebeca Grynspan, Guyana's Carolyn Rodrigues-Birkett and Macky Sall of Senegal are all in the running to succeed outgoing Secretary-General Antonio Guterres.
"This UN town hall will ... provide a rare opportunity to engage with the ideas, priorities and leadership vision of the candidates for one of the world's most important positions," General Assembly President Annalena Baerbock said.
"In an increasingly fragmented world, the United Nations needs not only an exceptional chief administrator who will make the organisation fit for the 21st century but also the leadership and integrity of someone willing to vigorously defend the UN Charter and all its three pillars," she added.
The United Nations is facing a profound financial and confidence crisis, with US President Donald Trump's administration accusing it of spending recklessly, spreading itself too thin and failing to deliver satisfactory results.
The United States is therefore demanding a return to the organization's fundamental purposes of peace and security, while other states stress the equal importance of its other two pillars: human rights and development.
Against this backdrop, "the race does seem short on energy" compared with the livelier 2016 campaign, which had "more candidates and lots of intrigue" before Guterres was ultimately selected, International Crisis Group analyst Richard Gowan told AFP.
"This time around everyone agrees that the race is incredibly important for the future of the UN, but the contest itself has been quite cautious," he added.
"There is a sense that the candidates are treading carefully to avoid offending the US and other veto powers."
The candidates have already faced three hours of questioning each from representatives of the 193 UN member states.
Most stressed the need to continue the organisation's ongoing reforms to make it more efficient, as well as the importance of greater involvement by the secretary-general in peace efforts around the world.
On July 30, the 15 members of the Security Council are due to hold their first closed-door, anonymous vote on their preferences among the candidates.
That first "straw poll" will be followed by an unspecified number of similar votes until one candidate can secure the required nine votes without a veto from any of the five permanent members.
The name of the selected candidate will then be sent to the General Assembly for the formal appointment to the position.
He or she will take office on Jan 1, 2027.
Several diplomats interviewed by AFP were cautious about the outcome of the race, saying a number of capitals had yet to make their choice.
"I find that Grossi is still the mostly frequently mentioned front-runner, but he has not locked the race up. Other candidates like Grynspan and Rodrigues-Birkett also have significant numbers of admirers," Gowan said, noting that a dark horse candidate could still emerge.
Many names are still circulating in UN corridors and the media, notably that of FIFA president Gianni Infantino, whom Trump would reportedly like to see take the helm of the UN, according to the New York Post.
Under a tradition of geographic rotation that is not always followed, Latin America is making a claim to the post this time.
Many states are also calling for a woman to hold the position for the first time.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Takiyuddin: Viral memo urging vote for BN is fake
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pas secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan has denied instructing party members to vote for Barisan Nasional (BN) in the Negri Sembilan elections.
Takiyuddin said a media statement circulating online, purportedly bearing such instructions along with the Pas letterhead and his name, is fake.
The fabricated statement also instructed party members to ensure their family members vote for BN to secure Pakatan Harapan's defeat in the state polls.
It further urged Pas members to refrain from raising the "47 Yayasan Akalbudi cases", which likely refers to Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi's discharge not amounting to an acquittal (DNAA) on 47 corruption-related charges.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Deadly wildfires force thousands from homes in parts of Europe
Article:
ROME: Wildfires swept across parts of Italy, France and Spain on Wednesday, killing three firefighters and forcing thousands to flee as scorching temperatures and strong winds fuelled the blazes.
The fires come as southern Europe endures another spell of severe heat, with temperatures topping 40C in some areas.
Scientists agree that climate change caused by humans burning fossil fuels is making extreme weather events such as fires and heatwaves more likely and more severe.
In Sicily, around 6,000 firefighters, forest guards and civil protection officials battled dozens of fires that have raged for days across the Mediterranean island.
Regional president Renato Schifani said about 50 fires were still burning despite the deployment of water-bombing aircraft.
Interior Minister Matteo Piantedosi said a firefighter died after falling ill while battling the flames.
A spokesperson for the Italian Civil Protection Agency told AFP the situation in Sicily was "serious" and that the fires were "being fuelled by very high temperatures and very dry soil."
More than 160 fires have also been recorded in the neighbouring Calabria region which the head of local civil protection said were deliberate cases of arson by "malicious individuals" who had among other things tied rags soaked in flammable liquid to the tails of stray cats to spread fires.
In France, two firefighters died on Tuesday tackling a blaze that broke out near Bordeaux airport.
France's Interior Minister Laurent Nunez met the firefighters' families on Wednesday, as unions warned workers were facing "exhaustion" and a lack of resources.
Two raging fires displaced thousands of people in the country's southwest and one ravaged around 2,000 hectares in the picturesque Arcachon Basin region near Bordeaux.
"We're dealing with a very dense stand of closely packed pines," firefighter Captain Wilfried Schneider told AFP, describing a fire of "rare" intensity.
- BIG BLACK CLOUD -
"We saw a big cloud of black smoke spread across the sky in the middle of the afternoon," said Julie Leonard, a Bordeaux resident in her thirties who came with her family to the affected Le Porge area but in a spot not under threat.
"If I'd been brave, I would have stayed in my cabin, but it was too hot. I got in my car and left," said Bernard Roche, 75, who has lived in the area for 35 years.
"In the forest, it's been particularly dry these last few days," the former lifeguard said.
Le Porge's mayor Martial Zaninetti said the fire could have been started by a machine used to clear forest track but this is yet to be officially confirmed.
Around 700 people left their homes as a precaution in Le Porge, where more than 3,000 campers were also relocated, the prefecture said.
A blaze that raced through wooded hills in another part of southern France, forcing hundreds from their homes and devouring an area twice the size of London's Heathrow airport, was being contained earlier Wednesday, officials said.
The fire erupted Tuesday in the Var region and spread at an unexpectedly fast pace, forcing some 400 people to evacuate and devouring 2,550 hectares.
Nearly 1,000 firefighters and specialised water aircraft battled to contain the blaze in the region that lies along France's Mediterranean coast between the cities of Marseille and Nice.
In total, 12,000 people have been evacuated in southwest France and the Gironde Prefect Sophie Brocas has called for hundreds more to leave.
In Spain, authorities ordered the evacuation of several districts on Wednesday around the city of Toledo, south of Madrid, after a major wildfire threatened homes.
Emergency alerts were sent by telephone to residents of the districts because of the fire and several arterial roads were cut off, the Civil Guard said.
Emergency services said that "due to the progression" of the fire, three villages had been ordered evacuated and people in a fourth had been told to stay in their homes with doors and windows closed "until further notice."
Toledo is 75km from the capital.
The new blaze spread as hundreds of firefighters battled a major wildfire that has devastated  32,000 hectares about 100km north of Madrid.
In 2025, more than 393,000 hectares had been consumed by flames in Spain, according to the European Forest Fire Information System, the worst toll in the country's recent history.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Stranger buys treats for children while celebrating daughter's pregnancy
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A man who bought snacks and ice cream for children at a convenience store was celebrating his daughter's pregnancy after years of waiting, according to a social media post.
The incident, shared by Threads user @sashrinda, happened when she and her children stopped at a convenience store after having steamboat skewers from a nearby food truck.
In the post, she said her children were choosing their food when the man, whom she referred to as "uncle", approached them and gave each child a KitKat.
She said the man later returned to the store and surprised the children by buying each of them an ice cream and a box of Milo drinks.
In a video shared alongside the post, the man explained that he was giving out the treats because his daughter, who had been married for five years, was finally expecting her first child and was four months pregnant.
The gesture was his way of sharing his happiness and celebrating the long-awaited news with others.
"Listen to why uncle did this. May your child safely deliver your first grandchild. May you live a long life and always be healthy, uncle," she wrote.
The woman later shared that the man's daughter had contacted her through direct message after the post gained attention online.
She said the man was originally from Penang and had travelled to Johor Bahru before working in Singapore, adding that his kindness may have come from missing his family, who lived far away.
"A man who left home to earn a living for his family. Thank you to everyone who helped repost until his daughter noticed," she wrote.
The daughter also thanked social media users for their well wishes and prayers for her pregnancy.
The post has since received attention from social media users, with many praising the man's generosity and the simple act of kindness that brightened the children's day.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia directs UNHCR to stop refugee registration for now
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government has directed the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) to halt all refugee registrations temporarily to allow for the restructuring of the management system via the Refugee Registration Document (DPP) programme.
Deputy Foreign Minister Datuk Lukanisman Awang Sauni said the decision was made by the Cabinet to ensure that the management and registration of refugees in the country are carried out in a more orderly manner under government supervision.
"We have gone through the Cabinet's decision and instructed the UNHCR to stop any refugee registrations for the time being because we are reorganising the implementation of the DPP programme.
"Through the DPP, the government can manage data in a more organised manner, including the purposes of data coordination, identity verification, monitoring, and regulation without relying on the records of any foreign entities," he said when answering a supplementary question from Senator Hussin Ismail in the Dewan Negara today.
Hussin was seeking an explanation regarding the government's measures to address the issue of Rohingya refugees who possess neither identity documents nor citizenship recognised by their country of origin, Myanmar.
Lukanisman said that registration under the DPP will be carried out through a strict process in collaboration with various enforcement agencies to ensure that only eligible individuals are registered and to prevent the entry of illegal immigrants in the future.
He said Malaysia is also continuing a multilateral approach towards Myanmar and Asean member countries to address the Rohingya refugee issue more seriously.
He said that the step is important to prevent Malaysia from continuing to bear the burden of refugee management since the country is not a signatory to the 1951 Refugee Convention, in addition to considering the impact on the local community.
Meanwhile, Lukanisman said that around 5,000 Myanmar nationals currently in the immigration detention depot will be repatriated following the readiness of the country's government to accept them back.
"Approximately 5,000 Myanmar nationals who are currently detained at the depot will be repatriated to the Myanmar government and will be sent back using naval ships, subject to the resolution process," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ACCCIM calls for tougher forced labour rules
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Associated Chinese Chambers of Commerce and Industry of Malaysia (ACCCIM) has urged the government to strengthen laws and enforcement to stop imports linked to forced labour.
This is to address a regulatory gap identified by the United States Trade Representative (USTR) related to forced labour.
ACCCIM think tank Socio-Economic Research Centre executive director Lee Heng Guie said Malaysian exporters would inevitably feel the impact of the proposed tariff, given that the US remains one of the country's largest export markets.
However, he noted that the 10 per cent tariff rate proposed on Malaysia is lower than the 12.5 per cent rate facing several regional peers including Singapore, the Philippines, Thailand and Vietnam.
"Malaysia will be impacted, but compared with some other Asean countries, we are still at the lower end.
"The original reciprocal tariff rate was 19 per cent, so the proposed 10 per cent rate offers some relief," he said during a media briefing today.
His remarks followed a USTR report released on June 2, which concluded that Malaysia had failed to impose and effectively enforce a forced labour import prohibition, resulting in a proposed additional 10 per cent tariff on imports from the country.
Malaysia falls under the lower 10 per cent tariff category due to commitments undertaken under its Agreement on Reciprocal Trade relating to forced labour import prohibitions.
Lee said exporters could face greater scrutiny if there is evidence of forced labour practices within their operations or supply chains.
This includes the sourcing of intermediate inputs from third countries linked to forced labour concerns.
"The 10 per cent tariff offers some comfort and can be viewed more positively compared with the rates imposed on some neighbouring countries.
"Companies will have to strengthen surveillance of their supply chains and ensure suppliers comply with forced labour regulations. Evidence, audits and proper checks will become increasingly important," he said.
However, he said the chamber welcomed the government's response to the issue.
He noted that the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry is actively working to address the matter while maintaining that Malaysia already has comprehensive domestic laws against forced labour.
He said the ministry has acknowledged a legal gap, namely the absence of an import prohibition law to screen goods and raw materials produced with forced labour, despite existing laws against forced labour practices.
"The US remains a major trading partner for Malaysia, so continued engagement between the ministry and the USTR will be important in addressing concerns raised under the Section 301 investigation," he said.
Lee emphasised the need for closer collaboration between the government and industry to strengthen supply chain oversight.
"Both the government and manufacturers and suppliers should collaborate to introduce a verification and traceability mechanism to ensure imported goods and raw materials are not linked to forced labour practices," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thailand assures Malaysians of safety amid southern border attacks
Article:
KOTA BARU: Thailand has assured Malaysians that security remains a top priority in its southern border provinces despite ongoing insurgent attacks, including incidents in areas bordering Malaysia.
Thailand's Consul-General in Kelantan, Phasit Chudabuddhi, said Thai security authorities had stepped up monitoring and would continue taking all necessary measures to ensure the safety of Malaysians travelling to the kingdom.
He said the attacks were not targeted at Malaysian visitors.
"I would like to stress that all the incidents were not targeted at foreign tourists, including those from Malaysia.
"All Thai agencies have stepped up security measures to prevent untoward incidents," he told reporters this after paying a courtesy call on Malaysia Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) Rantau Panjang commander Siti Khadijah Hamzah yesterday.
He was commenting on last month's car bomb attack in Narathiwat, which injured two Malaysians.
Phasit said the actual targets of those causing unrest were Thai security forces.
He said Thai authorities remained committed to ensuring the safety and security of all visitors, including Malaysians travelling to southern Thailand.
Siti Khadijah said the meeting discussed several bilateral issues, including strengthening cooperation in border control and facilitating smoother cross-border movement between Malaysia and Thailand.
She said both sides reaffirmed their commitment to maintaining close cooperation to ensure security and ease of travel along the Malaysia-Thailand border.
Meanwhile, the deep south recorded another deadly assault yesterday, which claimed the lives of five Thai Ranger officers.
The attack occurred at about 6.45pm (Thai time) when six armed insurgents dressed in black launched an assault on the Bu Ke Chami municipal checkpoint in Tanyong Mas, Ra-ngae district, Narathiwat, located about 65km from Rantau Panjang.
The attackers reportedly opened fire and hurled pipe bombs at personnel from the 4509th Ranger Company, killing five rangers and injuring two civilians, including a 10-year-old boy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BNP Paribas announces rocketing Q2 profit
Article:
FRANCE: French banking giant BNP Paribas on Thursday announced almost-record profits for the second quarter this year, driven by the sale of its stake in a Belgian insurance firm.
The bank said it had made 4.3 billion euros (US$4.9 billion) this quarter, hailing the performance of its asset management division, which was boosted by last year's acquisition of AXA IM.
The banking giant's net profit surged 33.4 per cent year-on-year, in line with expectations from analysts surveyed by financial news agency Bloomberg and data provider FactSet.
Its profits approaching a record set in 2023, were bolstered by a gain of around 850 million euros linked to the sale of a 25 per cent stake in AG Insurance through its Belgian subsidiary.
The bank is not yet reflecting a possible hit to its finances from a court case last October in the United States, where a jury found the firm liable for atrocities that took place under the rule of Sudan's Omar al-Bashir.
A jury awarded more than US$20 million in damages to three plaintiffs, a verdict the bank appealed against in May.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah strengthens efforts to manage human-crocodile conflict
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Sabah is adopting an integrated approach to address human-crocodile conflict by bringing together resources, expertise and personnel.
State Assistant Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Dr Andi Md Shamsureezal Mohd Saina said the approach was suited to Sabah's geographical landscape, which comprises extensive river systems, estuaries and coastal areas, as well as a wide distribution of conflict hotspots.
Sabah's extensive waterways and coastal areas have made human-crocodile conflict a recurring challenge, leading to stronger coordination among agencies.
On July 18, the body of a 57-year-old man who was attacked by a crocodile while cleaning a boat at Kampung Batu Putih was found. His 19-year-old son witnessed the attack and saw his father being dragged away by the reptile.
Earlier in April, a 5-year-old girl went missing after she was reportedly snatched by a crocodile at a beach near Jalan Felda Sahabat 16, Desa Kencana.
Dr Andi said since 2023, the state government had strengthened mitigation efforts through the establishment of the Sabah State Crocodile Mitigation Operations Committee, chaired by the Sabah State Affairs Office secretary.
"The committee brings together the Sabah Wildlife Department, the police and other relevant agencies to coordinate operational responses, information sharing and rapid action in high-risk conflict areas.
"Among the measures that have been and continue to be implemented are integrated operations, the management of high-risk crocodiles based on assessments by the Sabah Wildlife Department and relevant legal provisions, public awareness programmes, the installation of warning signboards, and technical collaboration with relevant experts to manage and reduce human-crocodile conflict in Sabah.
"The approach adopted in Sabah is based on the location and level of conflict risk identified. Unlike the permanent deployment of special task forces along rivers practised in some other states," he said during the state legislative assembly sitting here.
He was replying to Datuk Wong Hong Jun (Warisan-Tanjung Aru) on the government's action plan to address human-crocodile conflict and whether it would emulate Sarawak in establishing a special task force stationed along rivers to capture crocodiles.
For the longer term, under the 13th Malaysia Plan, Dr Andi said the state government would implement comprehensive wildlife conflict management covering elephants, monkeys and other wildlife species.
He added that the approach focused on continuous monitoring, scientific research, community participation and strengthening the capacity of implementing agencies to ensure wildlife conflict management was carried out in a more sustainable and effective manner.
"The state government will continue to strengthen the management of human-crocodile conflict through a science-based approach, continuous monitoring, effective operational responses, inter-agency collaboration and community involvement to ensure public safety while preserving Sabah's biodiversity."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Customs collects RM43.62b revenue in first half of 2026
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Customs Department collected RM43.62 billion in revenue from January to June this year, exceeding the Finance Ministry's half-year target of RM40 billion.
Its director-general Datuk Amran Ahmad said the collection surpassed the target by RM3.62 billion, or 109.05 per cent, and was 22.22 per cent higher than the RM35.69 billion recorded during the same period last year.
He said the strong performance reflected the department's continued commitment to its three core functions, collecting national revenue, facilitating trade and enforcing the law to safeguard the country's revenue, economy and national interests.
"To sustain this momentum, the department will continue strengthening risk management, compliance audits and targeted operations through the use of data analytics, technology and more effective profiling," he said at a press conference on the department's revenue collection and enforcement achievements for the January-June 2026 period here today.
Amran said enforcement against high-risk commodities, abuse of tax incentives and non-compliance would be intensified to prevent revenue leakages and help achieve the department's RM80 billion revenue collection target for 2026.
In a related development, he said Customs uncovered 3,609 cases involving attempted smuggling and customs duty and tax evasion during the first six months of the year.
He said the total value of seizures, including duties and taxes, amounted to RM1.30 billion, a 21.5 per cent increase from RM1.07 billion recorded during the same period last year.
"Drug-related commodities recorded the highest seizure value, involving 220 cases and 2,750.11kg as well as 3,399.10 litres of various narcotics worth RM366.58 million," he said.
Amran said cigarettes accounted for the highest number of cases, with 1,494 cases involving 1.24 billion sticks and total seizures, including duties and taxes, valued at RM329.27 million.
He said the department also seized 2,026 vehicles in 511 cases, with a total value of RM191.07 million, while 417 cases involved 1.79 million litres of alcoholic beverages worth RM81.41 million.
"For waste and scrap commodities, 104 cases were recorded with seizures valued at RM40.13 million, while other categories, including firecrackers and fireworks, rice, frozen products, protected species (CITES), textiles, tyres, vehicle spare parts and currency, accounted for 863 cases," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Abang Johari: AirBorneo’s first international flight marks new era for Sarawak as regional economic hub
Article:
SIBU, July 23 — AirBorneo’s inaugural international flight from Kuching to Singapore on Wednesday marked a historic milestone in Sarawak’s aviation development, said Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg.
The Premier said this during the Sarawak Day 2026 Celebration at Dataran Tun Tuanku Haji Bujang here on Wednesday night.
“The second gift for the people of Sarawak that I wish to announce is that AirBorneo, our very own airline, is making its first international flight from Kuching to Singapore,” he said.
Abang Johari said the inaugural flight was more than the opening of a new route, as it marked the beginning of a new era in the development of Sarawak’s air connectivity within the region.
“It reflects our confidence in Sarawak’s ability to build a more efficient, competitive, and future-oriented air connectivity system,” he said.
He stressed AirBorneo was a manifestation of the Sarawak government’s commitment to strengthening connectivity, stimulating trade, expanding tourism, and attracting more investment in the state.
He said the airline would also provide greater opportunities for Sarawakians and the business community to connect with wider markets.
Abang Johari also envisioned AirBorneo helping to promote Sarawak’s unique local products to international travellers, including dabai (Sarawak olive) from Song and Kanowit.
“Perhaps in the future, AirBorneo will have dabai from Song and Kanowit on its menu for international routes such as Kuching-Singapore, so that our unique products can be known to the world,” he said.
He added that AirBorneo would complement the modern transport ecosystem being developed in Sarawak, including ports, road networks, airports, and the Autonomous Rapid Transit (ART) system.
“These initiatives complement one another in creating a more efficient, sustainable, and integrated mobility network,” he said.
Abang Johari said the launch of AirBorneo’s international operations was in line with Sarawak’s broader aspiration to strengthen its position as a regional economic hub and contribute more significantly to Malaysia’s development.
The Premier also noted that the international flight came just two days after AirBorneo commenced its inaugural flight from Kuching to Kuala Lumpur on July 20, marking another significant step in the airline’s development. — The Borneo Post

Title: AirBorneo launches first international route with Kuching-Singapore flight
Article:
KUCHING, July 23 — AirBorneo Airways (AirBorneo) launched its first international route on Sarawak Day, connecting Kuching and Singapore through Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 4.
Marking the airline’s entry into international operations, Flight MY363 departed Kuching at 6.50pm and arrived in Singapore at 8.04pm, 21 minutes ahead of schedule, said AirBorneo in a statement yesterday.
The return flight, MY364, departed Singapore at 9.27pm and arrived in Kuching at 10.36pm, 14 minutes ahead of schedule.
AirBorneo chairman Datuk Amar Mohamad Abu Bakar Marzuki said the inaugural international route marked a significant milestone for both the airline and Sarawak.
“It reflects our commitment to strengthening connectivity, expanding opportunities and supporting Sarawak’s long-term economic aspirations.
“Singapore is an important regional gateway, and this new route will strengthen tourism, trade, investment and people-to-people connections between Sarawak and Singapore,” he said.
“As we celebrate Sarawak Day, this milestone reflects Sarawak’s continued growth and our shared vision of positioning the state as a dynamic gateway to the region,” he added.
AirBorneo chief executive officer Megat Ardian Aminuddin said the launch of the airline’s first international route marked an important milestone in its next phase of growth.
“Singapore is a natural choice for our first international destination, reflecting the strong connectivity and close economic ties between Sarawak and one of the region’s key aviation and commercial hubs.
“This route marks the beginning of our international expansion as we continue to grow our network strategically across the region,” he said.
He added that they look forward to connecting Sarawak with more destinations, strengthening regional connectivity and supporting the state’s long-term growth.
AirBorneo will operate daily flights between Kuching and Singapore. Tickets for the route are on sale via the AirBorneo website and mobile app. — The Borne Post

Title: Umno leaders cool to Hamzah Zainudin’s ‘Perikatan Barisan Nasional’ proposal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Several Umno leaders have expressed reservations over Opposition Leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin’s proposal to name the Barisan Nasional (BN)-Perikatan Nasional (PN) cooperation “Perikatan Barisan Nasional”.
Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail described the proposed name as irrelevant, saying it did not reflect the nature of the current arrangement, which is not the formation of a new political coalition, Malay daily
Sinar Harian
reported today.
Instead, he said the BN-PN partnership was merely an understanding to contest the Negeri Sembilan state election together.
“His (Hamzah’s) statement is outside the scope of what we had discussed previously. To me, the term is not relevant because this is only a temporary cooperation. I also do not know what his interpretation is,” he told reporters after a state election campaign event in Palong 4, Jempol, last night.
Umno secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, who was also present, stressed that BN’s leadership had never discussed or approved the proposed name.
He said the cooperation with PN was based solely on an understanding to avoid multi-cornered contests and maximise their chances of winning the Negeri Sembilan election.
“As for ‘Perikatan Barisan Nasional’, that is his statement. We, as the leadership, have never agreed to it.
“Let us work together with mutual understanding, avoid overlapping seats and win the Negeri Sembilan state election together.
“If we can cooperate well, God willing, Negeri Sembilan will become the starting point and catalyst for the unity long desired by the Muslim community as a whole,” he said.
Asyraf also urged all parties to embrace the cooperation sincerely so the alliance could be strengthened and govern the state effectively if given the mandate by voters.
Meanwhile, Negeri Sembilan Umno chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the name of the BN-PN cooperation was not something that should be a priority.
“That is merely Hamzah’s proposal and it will be determined by the top leadership of both parties.
“But I do not think it is a major issue or something we should be focusing on,” he said.

Title: Tanjong Sepat to be free of pig farms by October, says Selangor exco
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — The clearance of all pig farms in Tanjong Sepat, Kuala Langat is expected to be completed by October, bringing pig farming operations in the area to an end.
Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the timeline would allow the remaining piglets in the area to reach maturity at seven to eight months of age before being disposed of, Kosmo! Online reported.
He said about 12,000 pigs remain in Tanjong Sepat, down from around 240,000 before the phased clearance exercise began.
“We decided in February to allow the remaining piglets to be disposed of once they reach the appropriate age of seven to eight months.
“This means the clearance process is expected to be completed around September or October. After that, the exercise will be fully concluded,” he told reporters today.
Earlier, Izham officiated the review workshop for the Selangor Integrated Coastal Management Plan.
He said the Selangor Department of Veterinary Services (DVS) is conducting an audit to update the actual number of pigs still remaining in the area.
“The clearance is being carried out in stages to give farmers time to manage their remaining livestock, unless a disease outbreak is detected that requires immediate seizure.
“I am confident the latest DVS audit will show the number has fallen further, possibly by half,” he said.
Izham also said the state government has instructed the DVS and appointed consultants to assess the level of soil contamination in the area in preparation for rehabilitation works.
